parts.title: SKILL I: FOCUS ON THE LAST LINE

questions.passage:
(woman) How soon will you be leaving?
(man)   I'm on my way now.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He is leaving now.

reason.en: The man says 'I'm on my way now,' which directly means he is leaving at this moment. This aligns with the question 'How soon will you be leaving?' and is a direct interpretation of the last line.
reason.id: Pria itu berkata 'I'm on my way now,' yang secara langsung berarti dia pergi saat ini. Ini sesuai dengan pertanyaan 'How soon will you be leaving?' dan merupakan interpretasi langsung dari baris terakhir.

options.text: He has to go' out of his way.

reason.en: The phrase 'on my way' does not imply going out of one's way; it simply means traveling towards a destination. This option misinterprets the idiom.
reason.id: Frasa 'on my way' tidak menyiratkan harus keluar dari jalan; itu hanya berarti bepergian menuju tujuan. Pilihan ini salah menafsirkan idiom tersebut.

options.text: He will not be leaving soon.

reason.en: The man's statement 'I'm on my way now' directly contradicts the idea that he will not be leaving soon. He is leaving immediately.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu 'I'm on my way now' secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia tidak akan segera pergi. Dia pergi segera.

options.text: He will do it his own way.

reason.en: The phrase 'on my way' has no connection to the idiom 'do it his own way.' This option is irrelevant to the dialogue.
reason.id: Frasa 'on my way' tidak ada hubungannya dengan idiom 'do it his own way.' Pilihan ini tidak relevan dengan dialog tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(man)   Was Steve able to get into the house?
(woman) I left the door unlocked for him.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN ASSUME STEVE DID?

options.text: He locked the door.

reason.en: The woman states she left the door unlocked, implying Steve did not need to lock it. This option contradicts the dialogue.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyatakan dia membiarkan pintu tidak terkunci, menyiratkan Steve tidak perlu menguncinya. Pilihan ini bertentangan dengan dialog.

options.text: He tried unsuccessfully to get into the house.

reason.en: The woman's statement implies she expects Steve to enter the house. There is no indication he tried unsuccessfully.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita itu menyiratkan dia mengharapkan Steve masuk ke rumah. Tidak ada indikasi dia mencoba dengan tidak berhasil.

options.text: He was able to open the door.

reason.en: The woman left the door unlocked for Steve, which means she assumes he would be able to enter the house. This is a direct inference from the last line.
reason.id: Wanita itu membiarkan pintu tidak terkunci untuk Steve, yang berarti dia berasumsi Steve akan dapat masuk ke rumah. Ini adalah kesimpulan langsung dari baris terakhir.

options.text: He left the house without locking the door.

reason.en: The dialogue focuses on Steve getting into the house, not on him leaving or locking the door. This option is irrelevant to the conversation.
reason.id: Dialog berfokus pada Steve masuk ke rumah, bukan pada dia pergi atau mengunci pintu. Pilihan ini tidak relevan dengan percakapan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(man)   The dinner special is roast turkey with mashed potatoes and gravy, and apple pie for dessert.
(woman) That doesn't sound good to me.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She doesn't like to listen to turkeys.

reason.en: The woman is talking about the dinner special, not about listening to turkeys. This answer misinterprets the word 'turkey'. This relates to Skill 3: Avoid Similar Sounds.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut berbicara tentang menu spesial makan malam, bukan tentang mendengarkan kalkun. Jawaban ini salah menafsirkan kata 'turkey'. Ini berkaitan dengan Skill 3: Hindari Bunyi yang Mirip.

options.text: She thinks the dinner sounds special.

reason.en: The woman explicitly states 'That doesn't sound good to me,' which directly contradicts the idea that she thinks the dinner sounds special. This relates to Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Itu tidak terdengar enak bagiku,' yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia menganggap makan malam itu spesial. Ini berkaitan dengan Skill 1: Fokus pada Kalimat Terakhir.

options.text: She especially likes the roast turkey.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'That doesn't sound good to me' indicates a general dislike for the entire meal, not a specific preference for the roast turkey. This relates to Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'Itu tidak terdengar enak bagiku' menunjukkan ketidaksukaan umum terhadap seluruh hidangan, bukan preferensi spesifik untuk daging kalkun panggang. Ini berkaitan dengan Skill 1: Fokus pada Kalimat Terakhir.

options.text: She'd prefer a different dinner.

reason.en: The woman's last line, 'That doesn't sound good to me,' directly implies that she does not find the offered dinner appealing and would prefer something else. This relates to Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line.
reason.id: Kalimat terakhir wanita, 'Itu tidak terdengar enak bagiku,' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak menganggap hidangan yang ditawarkan menarik dan lebih memilih sesuatu yang lain. Ini berkaitan dengan Skill 1: Fokus pada Kalimat Terakhir.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(woman)Could you help me with my physics homework tonight? I'm really havingtrouble with it.
(man)  Sorry, I'm busy tonight.
questions.question: 4.WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY?

options.text: He'll be busy with her homework tonight.

reason.en: The man says he is busy tonight, which implies he cannot help her tonight. Option 0 is incorrect because it states he will be busy with *her* homework, which is not stated. He is busy with his own things.
reason.id: Pria itu mengatakan dia sibuk malam ini, yang menyiratkan dia tidak bisa membantunya malam ini. Opsi 0 salah karena menyatakan dia akan sibuk dengan pekerjaan rumah *nya*, yang tidak disebutkan. Dia sibuk dengan urusannya sendiri.

options.text: He can't help her tonight.

reason.en: The man's response 'Sorry, I'm busy tonight' directly implies that he is unable to help her at that specific time. This aligns with the skill of focusing on the last line to understand the implication.
reason.id: Respons pria itu 'Maaf, saya sibuk malam ini' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak dapat membantunya pada waktu tertentu itu. Ini selaras dengan keterampilan berfokus pada baris terakhir untuk memahami implikasinya.

options.text: He's sorry he can't ever help her.

reason.en: The man only states he is busy *tonight*. There is no information to suggest he can never help her. This option goes beyond the information given.
reason.id: Pria itu hanya menyatakan dia sibuk *malam ini*. Tidak ada informasi yang menunjukkan dia tidak pernah bisa membantunya. Opsi ini melampaui informasi yang diberikan.

options.text: He'll help her with her physics.

reason.en: The man explicitly states he is busy and cannot help. This option contradicts his statement.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan dia sibuk dan tidak bisa membantu. Opsi ini bertentangan dengan pernyataannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(man)   What did you think of Professor Martin's lecture on the migratory habits of whales?
(woman) I couldn't keep my eyes open.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Her eyes hurt.

reason.en: The woman says 'I couldn't keep my eyes open,' which is an idiom meaning she was very tired or bored, not that her eyes physically hurt.
reason.id: Wanita itu berkata 'I couldn't keep my eyes open,' yang merupakan idiom yang berarti dia sangat lelah atau bosan, bukan bahwa matanya sakit secara fisik.

options.text: She thought the lecture was great.

reason.en: The idiom 'couldn't keep my eyes open' implies the opposite of finding something great; it suggests boredom or extreme tiredness.
reason.id: Idiom 'couldn't keep my eyes open' menyiratkan kebalikan dari menemukan sesuatu yang hebat; itu menunjukkan kebosanan atau kelelahan yang ekstrem.

options.text: The class was boring.

reason.en: The phrase 'I couldn't keep my eyes open' is a common idiom used to express that something was very boring or made the speaker very sleepy.
reason.id: Frasa 'I couldn't keep my eyes open' adalah idiom umum yang digunakan untuk mengungkapkan bahwa sesuatu sangat membosankan atau membuat pembicara sangat mengantuk.

options.text: She didn't want to watch Professor Martin.

reason.en: The idiom 'couldn't keep my eyes open' refers to the lecture itself being boring, not a personal aversion to Professor Martin.
reason.id: Idiom 'couldn't keep my eyes open' mengacu pada kuliah itu sendiri yang membosankan, bukan keengganan pribadi terhadap Profesor Martin.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(woman)Have this month's bills been paid, or is that something we need to take care of now?
(man)  I paid the phone and electricity, but not the credit cards.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Not all the bills have been paid.

reason.en: The man states he paid some bills but not the credit cards, meaning not all bills are paid.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan bahwa dia membayar beberapa tagihan tetapi bukan kartu kredit, yang berarti tidak semua tagihan telah dibayar.

options.text: They don't have enough credit to pay the bills.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention anything about credit limits or lack thereof.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan apa pun tentang batas kredit atau kekurangannya.

options.text: What she said on the phone was not credible.

reason.en: The dialogue is about paying bills, not about phone conversations or credibility.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang membayar tagihan, bukan tentang percakapan telepon atau kredibilitas.

options.text: He used a credit card to pay some of the bills.

reason.en: The man explicitly states he did *not* pay the credit cards, implying he did not use them for payment in this context.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia *tidak* membayar kartu kredit, menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak menggunakannya untuk pembayaran dalam konteks ini.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(man)  Will you be able to get back from running all your errands by four o'clock?
(woman) I'll be back as quickly as I can.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY THAT SHE'LL DO?

options.text: She'll call back quickly.

reason.en: The woman says she will be back, not that she will call back.
reason.id: Wanita itu mengatakan dia akan kembali, bukan bahwa dia akan menelepon kembali.

options.text: She'll definitely be back by 4:00.

reason.en: The woman says she will be back 'as quickly as I can,' which is not a definite promise to be back by 4:00.
reason.id: Wanita itu mengatakan dia akan kembali 'secepat mungkin', yang bukan janji pasti untuk kembali pada jam 4:00.

options.text: She'll give it back by 4:00.

reason.en: The woman is talking about returning from errands, not giving something back.
reason.id: Wanita itu berbicara tentang kembali dari urusan, bukan mengembalikan sesuatu.

options.text: She'll try to return fast.

reason.en: The phrase 'as quickly as I can' is a synonym for trying to return fast. This is directly implied by the last line of the dialogue.
reason.id: Frasa 'secepat mungkin' adalah sinonim untuk mencoba kembali dengan cepat. Ini tersirat langsung dari baris terakhir dialog.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(man)   Have you seen Tim? I really need to talk with him about the phone bill.
(woman) Well,... he was here just a minute ago.
questions.question: 8.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT TIM?

options.text: She hasn't seen Tim.

reason.en: The woman implies she saw Tim, contradicting this option.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyiratkan bahwa dia melihat Tim, bertentangan dengan pilihan ini.

options.text: Tim was there only for a moment.

reason.en: The woman says Tim 'was here just a minute ago,' not that he was there only for a moment. This option misinterprets the duration.
reason.id: Wanita itu mengatakan Tim 'baru saja ada di sini beberapa menit yang lalu,' bukan bahwa dia hanya ada di sana sebentar. Pilihan ini salah menafsirkan durasinya.

options.text: Tim was around a short time ago.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'he was here just a minute ago' directly supports the idea that Tim was around a short time ago. This aligns with Skill 1, focusing on the last line.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita itu 'dia baru saja ada di sini beberapa menit yang lalu' secara langsung mendukung gagasan bahwa Tim ada di sekitar belum lama ini. Ini selaras dengan Skill 1, yang berfokus pada baris terakhir.

options.text: Tim will return in a minute.

reason.en: The dialogue does not provide any information about Tim's future return.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak memberikan informasi apa pun tentang kembalinya Tim di masa mendatang.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(man)  There's a car parked in my spot even though the sign says that this space is reserved.
(woman) I guess we'll have to park somewhere else.
questions.question: 9.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She doesn't like the place he chose.

reason.en: The woman's statement is a consequence of the man's statement about the reserved parking spot, not a personal preference about a location.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita adalah konsekuensi dari pernyataan pria tentang tempat parkir yang dikhususkan, bukan preferensi pribadi tentang suatu lokasi.

options.text: She doesn't want to get into the car.

reason.en: The dialogue is about parking a car, not about getting into it.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tentang memarkir mobil, bukan tentang masuk ke dalamnya.

options.text: She's glad the spot is reserved.

reason.en: The woman's statement implies they cannot park there, which is a negative outcome, not gladness about the reservation.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita menyiratkan bahwa mereka tidak dapat parkir di sana, yang merupakan hasil negatif, bukan kegembiraan tentang reservasi tersebut.

options.text: They can't park the car there.

reason.en: The man states the spot is reserved and someone is parked there. The woman's response, 'I guess we'll have to park somewhere else,' directly implies that they cannot park in the reserved spot.
reason.id: Pria menyatakan bahwa tempat itu dikhususkan dan seseorang parkir di sana. Respons wanita, 'Saya kira kita harus parkir di tempat lain,' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa mereka tidak dapat parkir di tempat yang dikhususkan tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(woman)Do we have enough food for all the guests who are attending the reception this evening?
(man)  The refrigerator's about to burst.
questions.question: 10.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: There's plenty to eat.

reason.en: The man's statement 'The refrigerator's about to burst' is an idiom meaning it is very full. This implies there is plenty of food.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria 'The refrigerator's about to burst' adalah idiom yang berarti sangat penuh. Ini menyiratkan ada banyak makanan.

options.text: The refrigerator's broken.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention the refrigerator being broken; it implies it is full.
reason.id: Dialog tidak menyebutkan kulkas rusak; itu menyiratkan kulkas itu penuh.

options.text: The food isn't in the refrigerator.

reason.en: The man's statement implies the food is in the refrigerator because it is full.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria menyiratkan makanan ada di kulkas karena kulkas itu penuh.

options.text: He's not sure if there's enough.

reason.en: The man's statement is a direct indication that there is a lot of food, not uncertainty.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria adalah indikasi langsung bahwa ada banyak makanan, bukan ketidakpastian.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(woman) What did you think of the final exam in algebra?
(man) It was too easy!
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The final exam was harder than the others.

reason.en: The man said the exam was 'too easy,' which is the opposite of 'harder than the others.'
reason.id: Pria itu mengatakan ujiannya 'terlalu mudah,' yang merupakan kebalikan dari 'lebih sulit dari yang lain.'

options.text: There were two exams rather than one.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention the number of exams.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan jumlah ujian.

options.text: He thought the exam would be easier.

reason.en: The man said the exam was 'too easy,' not that he expected it to be easier.
reason.id: Pria itu mengatakan ujiannya 'terlalu mudah,' bukan bahwa dia mengharapkannya lebih mudah.

options.text: The exam was not very difficult.

reason.en: The phrase 'too easy' directly implies that the exam was not very difficult.
reason.id: Frasa 'terlalu mudah' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa ujian tersebut tidak terlalu sulit.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(woman) How are you feeling today?
(man) I'm really feeling rather sick.
questions.question: 2. HOW IS THE MAN FEELING?

options.text: He's not feeling very well.

reason.en: The man states, 'I'm really feeling rather sick.' Option 0, 'He's not feeling very well,' is a synonym for 'rather sick,' which directly answers the question about how the man is feeling. This aligns with Skill 2, which emphasizes choosing answers with synonyms.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan, 'I'm really feeling rather sick.' Pilihan 0, 'He's not feeling very well,' adalah sinonim dari 'rather sick,' yang secara langsung menjawab pertanyaan tentang bagaimana perasaan pria itu. Ini sesuai dengan Skill 2, yang menekankan memilih jawaban dengan sinonim.

options.text: He's rather sick of working.

reason.en: The dialogue mentions the man is 'sick,' but not 'sick of working.' This option introduces an unrelated idea and does not reflect what the man said. It does not use synonyms for the man's stated feeling.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut menyebutkan pria itu 'sick' (sakit), tetapi tidak 'sick of working' (bosan bekerja). Pilihan ini memperkenalkan ide yang tidak berhubungan dan tidak mencerminkan apa yang dikatakan pria itu. Pilihan ini tidak menggunakan sinonim untuk perasaan pria yang dinyatakan.

options.text: He's feeling better today than yesterday.

reason.en: The dialogue does not provide any information about how the man felt yesterday or compare his current feelings to past feelings. This option is not supported by the text.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak memberikan informasi apa pun tentang bagaimana perasaan pria itu kemarin atau membandingkan perasaannya saat ini dengan perasaan di masa lalu. Pilihan ini tidak didukung oleh teks.

options.text: He'd really rather not answer the question.

reason.en: The man explicitly states he is feeling 'rather sick.' He does not express a desire to avoid answering the question. This option misinterprets his statement.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia merasa 'rather sick' (agak sakit). Dia tidak mengungkapkan keinginan untuk menghindari menjawab pertanyaan. Pilihan ini salah menafsirkan pernyataannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man) Has your family been in business for quite some time?
(woman) No, the family business was just estab lished last year.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT THE FAMILY BUSINESS?

options.text: The company was founded about a year ago.

reason.en: The woman states that the business 'was just established last year.' Option 0, 'The company was founded about a year ago,' uses the synonym 'founded' for 'established' and 'about a year ago' for 'last year,' accurately paraphrasing the woman's statement.
reason.id: Wanita menyatakan bahwa bisnis 'baru didirikan tahun lalu'. Pilihan 0, 'Perusahaan didirikan sekitar setahun yang lalu,' menggunakan sinonim 'didirikan' untuk 'established' dan 'sekitar setahun yang lalu' untuk 'tahun lalu,' yang secara akurat memparafrasekan pernyataan wanita tersebut.

options.text: It was just established that he could go into business.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the meaning of 'established' and adds information ('that he could go into business') not present in the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan arti 'established' dan menambahkan informasi ('bahwa dia bisa berbisnis') yang tidak ada dalam dialog.

options.text: The family is well-established.

reason.en: The dialogue states the business was established last year, implying it is new, not 'well-established,' which suggests a long history.
reason.id: Dialog menyatakan bahwa bisnis tersebut didirikan tahun lalu, menyiratkan bahwa bisnis tersebut baru, bukan 'mapan,' yang menyiratkan sejarah panjang.

options.text: The business only lasted a year.

reason.en: The dialogue says the business 'was just established last year,' meaning it started then. This option incorrectly suggests the business ended after only one year.
reason.id: Dialog mengatakan bisnis tersebut 'baru didirikan tahun lalu,' yang berarti bisnis tersebut dimulai saat itu. Opsi ini secara keliru menyarankan bahwa bisnis tersebut berakhir setelah satu tahun.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(woman) Did you have to wait at the airport for a long time?
(man) No, the plane landed right on sched ule.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He did not look at the right schedule.

reason.en: The man states the plane landed 'on schedule,' which means it was on time. This option suggests he looked at the wrong schedule, which is not supported by the dialogue.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan pesawat mendarat 'sesuai jadwal', yang berarti tepat waktu. Pilihan ini menyiratkan dia melihat jadwal yang salah, yang tidak didukung oleh dialog.

options.text: The plane landed in the right place.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the timing of the plane's arrival, not its location. 'On schedule' refers to time, not place.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut membahas waktu kedatangan pesawat, bukan lokasinya. 'Sesuai jadwal' merujuk pada waktu, bukan tempat.

options.text: The plane arrived on time.

reason.en: The man's last line, 'No, the plane landed right on schedule,' directly answers the question about waiting. 'On schedule' means arriving at the planned time, so the man means the plane arrived on time.
reason.id: Kalimat terakhir pria itu, 'Tidak, pesawat mendarat tepat sesuai jadwal,' secara langsung menjawab pertanyaan tentang menunggu. 'Sesuai jadwal' berarti tiba pada waktu yang direncanakan, jadi pria itu bermaksud pesawat tiba tepat waktu.

options.text: He had to wait for the plane to land.

reason.en: The man explicitly states he did *not* have to wait long, and the plane landed 'on schedule.' This option contradicts his statement.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan dia *tidak* harus menunggu lama, dan pesawat mendarat 'sesuai jadwal.' Pilihan ini bertentangan dengan pernyataannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
(man) Do you want to join me in the pool?
(woman) Oh, I'll just run and put my suit on.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY?

options.text: She'd rather go running.

reason.en: The woman says she will put her suit on, which implies she is going to swim, not go running. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
reason.id: Wanita itu mengatakan dia akan memakai pakaian renangnya, yang menyiratkan dia akan berenang, bukan berlari. Oleh karena itu, pilihan ini salah.

options.text: She doesn't want to go into the pool.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'I'll just run and put my suit on' indicates her intention to get ready to swim, not that she doesn't want to go into the pool. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'Saya akan lari dan memakai pakaian renang saya' menunjukkan niatnya untuk bersiap berenang, bukan bahwa dia tidak ingin masuk ke kolam. Oleh karena itu, pilihan ini salah.

options.text: She'll change clothes quickly and go swimming.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'I'll just run and put my suit on' implies that she will quickly change into her swimsuit and then join the man in the pool. This is a paraphrase of the situation.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'Saya akan lari dan memakai pakaian renang saya' menyiratkan bahwa dia akan segera berganti pakaian renang dan kemudian bergabung dengan pria itu di kolam. Ini adalah parafrase dari situasi tersebut.

options.text: She needs a sweatsuit to go running.

reason.en: The woman mentions putting on her 'suit,' which in this context refers to a swimsuit, not a sweatsuit for running. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
reason.id: Wanita itu menyebutkan memakai 'pakaian renangnya', yang dalam konteks ini merujuk pada pakaian renang, bukan pakaian olahraga untuk berlari. Oleh karena itu, pilihan ini salah.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
(man) Last night's fire burned the entire hillside.
(woman) At least the homes were saved.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The firefighters saved the homes for last.
